title: Graph Theory, Networks, Scheduling, and Routing · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Design routes and schedules for deliveries and shared services. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a network routing and scheduling analysis after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent vertices, edges, direction, weights, and attributes from a real system.
Analyze paths, cycles, components, reachability, and bottlenecks.
Use trees and spanning structures for hierarchy and low-cost connectivity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

stimulus.title: Network Tradeoffs case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: math intermediate graphs networks. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A globally shortest route cannot overload shared resources.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare efficiency, resilience, fairness, and capacity in network decisions. Artifact under review: a network routing and scheduling analysis.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Network Tradeoffs, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Network Tradeoffs applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare efficiency, resilience, fairness, and capacity in network decisions.
